blocks:
سُورة الجاثية

مكّيّة وهي 37 آية

سورة الجاثية

Makkan, the number of its verses is thirty-seven verses according to the Kufan count, six in the remaining counts. Its point of difference is the verse ** حم ** according to the Kufan count. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم (1) Ḥā, Mīm. (٢) تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتابِ مِنَ اللهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ. (2) The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٣) إِنَّ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَآياتٍ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (3) Indeed in the heavens and the earth there are signs for the faithful. Al-Qummi (said): And these are the stars, the sun, and the moon; and in the earth is what emerges from it of various types of vegetation for mankind and the beasts. (٤)* وَفِي خَلْقِكُمْ وَما يَبُثُّ مِنْ دابَّةٍ آياتٌ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (4) And in your creation and in the creatures He scatters are signs for a people who are certain. And it was read (وقرئ) with the nasb (بالنّصب) (accusative case). (٥) وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ وَما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ مِنَ السَّماءِ مِنْ رِزْقٍ ** (5) and in the alternation of the night and the day, and whatever provision Allah sends down from the sky meaning rain, which He named provision (رزقاً) because it is the cause (سببه) of it ** فَأَحْيا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها ** and revives the earth with it after its death, (meaning) its dryness (يبسها) وَتَصْرِيفِ الرِّياحِ ** and in the shifting of the winds by the variation of their directions (جهاتها) and conditions (أحوالها). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, it comes from every side, and sometimes it is hot, and sometimes it is cold. And among them are those that stir up the clouds, and among them are those that spread (the clouds) over the earth, and among them are those that pollinate (يلقح) the trees *آياتٌ

are signs And it was read (وقرئ) (as) وتصريف الرّيح (and the shifting of the wind - singular) لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ for a people who apply reason. Both readings (القراءتان) exist for this (phrase). It was said that perhaps the difference in the verse endings (الفواصل) is due to the difference in the signs (الآيات) regarding subtlety (الدقّة) and clarity (الظهور).

(٦) تِلْكَ آياتُ اللهِ (6) These are the signs of Allah Those signs are His proofs (دلائله). ** نَتْلُوها عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِّ We recite them to you with the truth. فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَ اللهِ وَآياتِهِ ** So in what discourse will they believe after Allah and His signs? Meaning, after the signs of Allah. And the precedence of the Name of Allah is for emphasis (لِلْمُبَالَغَة) and magnification (وَالتَّعْظِيم), just as in your saying: "Zayd and his generosity impressed me." Or, after the discourse (حَدِيث) of Allah, which is the Quran, will you believe (تُؤْمِنُونَ)? And it was read with the yā' (بالياء).

(٧) وَيْلٌ لِكُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ ** a liar (كذّاب) أَثِيمٍ ** one who commits much sin (كثير الإثم). (7) Woe to every sinful liar, (٨) يَسْمَعُ آياتِ اللهِ تُتْلى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ يُصِرُّ ** He persists/remains firm upon his disbelief (كفره). مُسْتَكْبِراً ** being arrogant regarding belief in the signs (الآيات). And the word ثُمَّ (then/moreover) is used to indicate the remoteness (استبعاد) of persisting (الإصرار) after hearing the signs (الآيات). كَأَنْ لَمْ يَسْمَعْها ** meaning: as if he were [like that]. فَبَشِّرْهُ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** due to his persistence (إصراره). (8) who hears the signs of Allah recited to him, yet persists arrogantly, as if he had not heard them. So inform him of a painful punishment.

(٩) وَإِذا عَلِمَ مِنْ آياتِنا شَيْئاً ** (9) And when he learns something of Our signs And when something reaches him and he knows that it is from them (Our signs). And al-Qummi (said): "When he sees," thus placing knowledge (العلم) in the place of sight (الرّؤية). اتَّخَذَها هُزُواً ** he takes them in derision. Meaning, all the signs (الآيات) or the thing (الشيء) because it is in the meaning of the sign (الآية). أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ For such there is a humiliating punishment because of that.

(١٠) مِنْ وَرائِهِمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَلا يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ ** (10) Hell is behind them, and what they have earned will not avail them and it will not repel ما كَسَبُوا ** anything, of wealth and children شَيْئاً ** nor will what they have taken as guardians besides Allah. of Allah's punishment وَلا مَا اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَوْلِياءَ ** of idols (الأصنام) and leaders (الرؤساء). And for them is a great punishment which they cannot bear (لا يتحمّلونه).

(١١) هذا هُدىً ** (11) This is a guidance, أي القرآن meaning: the Quran. وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَهُمْ عَذابٌ مِنْ رِجْزٍ أَلِيمٌ ** (11) and those who defy the signs of their Lord, there is a painful punishment of the worst kind for them. وقرئ اليم بالرّفع والرّجز أشدّ العذاب. And alīm (أَلِيمٌ) was read with the rafʿ (nominative case). And al-rijz (الرّجز) is the severest punishment. (١٢) اللهُ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْبَحْرَ لِتَجْرِيَ الْفُلْكُ فِيهِ بِأَمْرِهِ ** (12) It is Allah who has subdued the sea for you, so that the ships may sail in it by His command, بتسخيره وأنتم راكبوها by His subjugation, while you are riding them (the ships). وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (12) and so that you may seek of His bounty, بالتجارة والغوص والصيد وغيرها through trade (التجارة), diving (الغوص), hunting (الصيد), and other means. وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ ** (12) and that you may be grateful. هذه النعم. for these blessings (النعم).

(١٣) وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمْ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً ** (13) And He has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, all of it, by creating them beneficial for you, originating from Him, existing through Him. ** إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ ** Indeed, there are signs in that for a people who reflect. upon His creations (صنايعه).

(١٤) قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يَغْفِرُوا ** (14) Tell those who have faith to forgive meaning, tell them: forgive, so that they may forgive; that is, they should pardon (يعفوا) and overlook (يصفحوا) those who do not expect the days of Allah (أَيَّامَ اللهِ), meaning, they do not anticipate His occurrences (وقائعه) against His enemies, ** لِيَجْزِيَ قَوْماً بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** that He may requite a people for what they used to earn. And it was recited لنجزي (that We may requite) with the Nūn (النّون). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: He says to the Imams of Truth (أئمّة الحقّ): Do not supplicate against the Imams of Injustice (أئمّة الجور) until Allah is the One Who punishes them.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Tell those upon whom We have conferred favor (منّنا عليهم) with Our knowledge (بمعرفتنا) that they should inform those who do not know. And when they inform them, they have indeed forgiven them.

(١٥) مَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً فَلِنَفْسِهِ وَمَنْ أَساءَ فَعَلَيْها ** (15) Whoever acts righteously, it is for his own sake, and whoever does evil, it is to his own detriment. since the reward of the deed is for it (the soul) and the punishment is upon it. ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ ** and He will recompense you for your deeds. (١٦) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ الْكِتابَ ** (16) Certainly We gave the Children of Israel the Book, the Torah وَالْحُكْمَ ** and wisdom (الحكمة) or the resolution of disputes (فصل الخصومات) وَالنُّبُوَّةَ ** since the prophets (الأنبياء) were numerous among them, more so than among others وَرَزَقْناهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** from the delights (اللّذائذ) which Allah made lawful وَفَضَّلْناهُمْ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ** the people (عالَمِي) of their time.

(١٧) وَآتَيْناهُمْ بَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ ** (17) and We gave them manifest proofs of the matter. proofs concerning the matter of religion (الدين), and miracles (المعجزات) are included therein. And it was said (that they are) signs concerning the matter of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, announcing his truthfulness. فَمَا اخْتَلَفُوا ** concerning that matter إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ ** except after knowledge (العلم) came to them of the reality of the situation, بَغْياً بَيْنَهُمْ ** out of enmity (عداوة) and envy (حسداً) among themselves. إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِي بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فِيما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ** Your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. by holding them accountable (بالمؤاخذة) and giving recompense (والمجازاة).

(١٨) ثُمَّ جَعَلْناكَ عَلى شَرِيعَةٍ ** (18) Then We set you upon a law a way (طريقة) مِنَ الْأَمْرِ ** the matter of the religion (امر الدين) فَاتَّبِعْها وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know. the opinions of the ignorant ones that follow desires (آراء الجهّال التابعة للشهوات). It was said (قيل) that they are the chiefs of Quraysh who said to him: "Return to the religion of your forefathers."

(١٩) إِنَّهُمْ لَنْ يُغْنُوا عَنْكَ مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً ** (19) Indeed, they will not avail you anything against Allah, from what He intends for you. وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِياءُ بَعْضٍ ** (and indeed the wrongdoers are allies of one another) since the shared nature (الجنسيّة) is the cause of affiliation. (This is the case) even if you were to ally yourself with them by following their desires. وَاللهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ ** (and Allah is the Guardian of the Godwary) So, ally yourself with Allah through piety (التّقى) and following the Law (الشريعة). Al-Qummi said: This is an admonition (تأديب) directed at the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is intended for his community (أمّته).

(٢٠) هذا بَصائِرُ لِلنَّاسِ ** (20) This is an insight for mankind, Clear proofs (بيّنات) that enable them to see the path (وجه) of success (الفلاح). وَهُدىً ** and a guidance from error (الضلال). وَرَحْمَةٌ ** and a mercy from Allah. لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ ** for a people who are certain. who seek certainty (اليقين). (٢١) أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ اجْتَرَحُوا السَّيِّئاتِ ** (21) Do those who commit misdeeds suppose The (أم) is disjunctive (منقطعة), and the meaning of the interrogative (الهمزة) within it is a denial of the supposition (الحسبان). And al-ijtirāḥ (الاجتراح) means acquisition (الاكتساب). أَنْ نَجْعَلَهُمْ ** that We shall make them that We shall render them كَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ like those who have faith and do righteous deeds, like them. سَواءً مَحْياهُمْ وَمَماتُهُمْ ** their life and death being equal? And (سواء) was recited with the naṣb (النّصب) (accusative case). ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ.** Evil is the judgement that they make!

(٢٢) وَخَلَقَ اللهُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ وَلِتُجْزى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِما كَسَبَتْ (22) Allah created the heavens and the earth with the truth, and so that every soul may be recompensed for what it has earned, وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ ** and they will not be wronged. by the reduction of reward (ثواب) or the doubling of punishment (عذاب). (٢٣) أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلهَهُ هَواهُ ** (23) Have you regarded him who has taken his desire as his god, It was said that one of them would admire a stone and worship it, but if he saw one better than it, he would reject the first one for the second. And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Quraysh; whenever they desired (هَوَوْا) something, they worshipped it. He said: And after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, it applied to his companions who usurped the right of the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام and took a leader (إماماً) according to their desires (أهوائهم). وَأَضَلَّهُ اللهُ عَلى عِلْمٍ ** and whom Allah has led astray knowingly, and abandoned him, knowing his misguidance (ضلاله) and the corruption of the essence of his soul (جوهر روحه). وَخَتَمَ عَلى سَمْعِهِ وَقَلْبِهِ ** and set a seal on his hearing and his heart, so he does not care about admonitions (المواعظ) nor reflect upon the signs (الآيات). وَجَعَلَ عَلى بَصَرِهِ غِشاوَةً ** and put a covering on his sight? so he does not look with the eye of insight (الاستبصار) and consideration (الاعتبار). فَمَنْ يَهْدِيهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ اللهِ ** Who will guide him after Allah? after his misguidance (ضلاله). أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ. Will you not then take admonition?

** (٢٤) وَقالُوا ما هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيا ** (24) And they say, 'There is nothing but the life of this world. We die and we live,'

And they said: وَقالُوا ما هِيَ ** What is life ** إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا ** except this worldly life of ours ** نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيا ** which we are in. It was said** (meaning) we die, and others live, those who come after us.

And al-Qummi (said): This is a case of advancement and delay (مقدم ومؤخر), because the Materialists (الدهرية) did not affirm the Resurrection (البعث والنشور) after death. Rather, they said: We live and we die. ** وَما يُهْلِكُنا إِلَّا الدَّهْرُ ** except the passage of time (مرور الزّمان). ** وَما لَهُمْ بِذلِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ ** (And they have no knowledge of that; they only make conjectures) since they have no proof for it. Al-Qummi (said): So this is conjecture (ظنّ) and doubt (شَكّ).

And this Ayah was revealed concerning the Materialists (الدهرية), and it applies to those who did what they did after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله to the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام and to the People of his House عليهم السلام. Their faith was merely an affirmation (إقراراً) without confirmation (تصديق) due to fear of the sword and desire for wealth.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, in a narration concerning the aspects of disbelief (وجوه الكفر), He said: "As for the disbelief of denial (كفر الجحود), it is the denial of Lordship (الربوبيّة), and it is the saying of one who says there is no Lord, no Paradise, and no Fire. This is the saying of two types of heretics (الزّنادقة) who are called the Materialists (الدّهريّة). They are those who say: ** وَما يُهْلِكُنا إِلَّا الدَّهْرُ ** (And nothing destroys us except time). This is a religion they established for themselves based on their own preference (الاستحسان), without any verification (تثبّت) from them or confirmation (تحقيق) of anything they say. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ ** (They only make conjectures) that it is as they say."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, that He said: "Do not curse time (الدّهر), for Allah is time (الدّهر)." He said: And its interpretation (تأويله) is that the people of the Age of Ignorance (أهل الجاهليّة) used to attribute the destructive events and the descending calamities to time (الدهر), saying: 'Time did such-and-such.' And they used to curse time. So He عليه السلام said: "The Doer of these matters is Allah the Almighty, so do not curse their Doer."

And it was said that its meaning is that Allah is the Disposer (مصرف) and Manager (مدبره) of time. He said: And the first interpretation (الوجه الأوّل) is better, for their speech is full of that, attributing the actions of Allah to time (الدهر).

(٢٥) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا بَيِّناتٍ ** (25) And when Our manifest signs are recited to them — meaning, clear in indication (واضحات الدلالة) regarding that which contradicts their belief (معتقدهم) — ما كانَ حُجَّتَهُمْ ** — meaning, they had no hold (متشبّث) by which they could oppose them — إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا ائْتُوا بِآبائِنا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ. (their only argument is that they say, 'Bring our fathers back, if you are truthful.')

(٢٦) ** قُلِ اللهُ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يَجْمَعُكُمْ إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ** (26) Say, 'It is Allah who gives you life, then He makes you die, then He will gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt.' For indeed, whoever is capable of initiating creation (الإبداء) is capable of repeating it (الإعادة). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (26) But most people do not know. Due to the scarcity of their reflection (تفكّرهم) and the limitation of their sight (نظرهم) to what they perceive through the senses (يحسّونه).

(٢٧) وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (27) To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. This is a generalization of the power (القدرة) after its specification. وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَخْسَرُ الْمُبْطِلُونَ. And on the day when the Hour is established, the falsifiers will be losers on that day. (٢٨) وَتَرى كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جاثِيَةً ** (28) You will see every nation kneeling. It is said that this means gathered (مجتمعة), derived from al-juthwah (الجثوة), which is the group (الجماعة); or kneeling (باركة) and poised (مستوفزة) upon the knees (الركب). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning upon their knees (على ركبها). كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ تُدْعى إِلى كِتابِهَا ** Every nation will be summoned to its book. (Meaning) the scroll of its deeds (صحيفة أعمالها). And kull (كلّ) was read with the nasb (accusative case). الْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** Today you shall be requited for what you used to do. (This phrase is understood) based on the estimation of a saying (القول).

(29) هذا كِتابُنا (This is Our Book) It was said: He attributed the scrolls of their deeds to Himself because He commanded the scribes (الكتبة) to write their deeds therein. I say: And another interpretation (وجه آخر) for this will come shortly. يَنْطِقُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِالْحَقِ (which speaks against you with the truth.) It bears witness against you concerning what you did, without addition or omission. إِنَّا كُنَّا نَسْتَنْسِخُ (Indeed, We have been transcribing) — meaning, We cause the angels to write down (نستكتب الملائكة) — ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (what you used to do) — your deeds (أعمالكم).

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "Indeed, the Book has not spoken, nor will it ever speak. Rather, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is the one who speaks by means of the Book." Allah the Almighty said: هذا كِتابُنا يَنْطِقُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِالْحَقِ (This is Our Book which speaks against you with the truth.) It was said to Him: "We do not recite it this way." So He said: "By Allah, Gabriel descended with it upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله exactly like this, but it is among what has been distorted (حرّف) from the Book of Allah."

I say: It is as if He (peace be upon him) recited يُنْطَقُ (yunṭaq) with a ḍamma on the yā and a fatḥa on the ṭā (i.e., in the passive voice, meaning 'is made to speak').

Al-Qummi [from Him (peace be upon him) (variant reading)] and from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about ن وَالْقَلَمِ (Nūn. By the Pen) He said: "Indeed, Allah created the Pen from a tree in Paradise called al-Khuld (الخلد). Then He said to a river in Paradise: 'Be ink (مداداً).' So the river froze and became whiter than snow and sweeter than honey. Then He said to the Pen: 'Write.' It said: 'O Lord, what should I write?' He said: 'Write what was and what will be until the Day of Resurrection.' So the Pen wrote upon a parchment (رقّ) whiter than silver and purer than ruby. Then He folded it and placed it in a corner of the Throne (العرش). Then He sealed the mouth of the Pen, so it did not speak, and it will never speak. That is the Preserved Book (الكتاب المكنون) from which all copies (النسخ) originate. Are you not Arabs? How is it that you do not know the meaning of the speech (الكلام)? One of you says to his companion: 'Copy (انسخ) that book,' and it is not copied except from another book, from the original (الأصل). And that is His saying: إِنَّا كُنَّا نَسْتَنْسِخُ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (Indeed, We have been transcribing what you used to do)."

In Sa'd al-Su'ud, concerning the narrative of the two angels entrusted with the servant: When they intend to descend morning and evening, Israfil (إسرافيل) copies (ينسخ) the servant's deeds for them from the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ) and gives it to them. Then, when they ascend morning and evening with the servant's register (ديوان), Israfil compares it with the copies (النسخ) that He transcribed for them, so that it becomes clear that it was exactly as copied from Him.

(٣٠) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَيُدْخِلُهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ** (As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy) which includes Paradise ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْمُبِينُ ** (That is the manifest triumph.) because of its purity from impurities (الشوائب). (٣١) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَفَلَمْ تَكُنْ آياتِي تُتْلى عَلَيْكُمْ ** (But as for those who were faithless: 'Were My signs not recited to you?) meaning, that will be said to them, yet you were arrogant concerning faith in them, وَكُنْتُمْ قَوْماً مُجْرِمِينَ (and you were a criminal people) whose habit (عادتكم) is criminality (الاجرام). (٣٢) وَإِذا قِيلَ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌّ وَالسَّاعَةُ لا رَيْبَ فِيها ** (And when it was said, 'Allah's promise is true, and there is no doubt in the Hour,') And it was recited with the accusative case (بالنّصب) قُلْتُمْ ما نَدْرِي مَا السَّاعَةُ إِنْ نَظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنًّا وَما نَحْنُ بِمُسْتَيْقِنِينَ. (you said, 'We do not know what the Hour is. We only make a conjecture, and we are not certain.') (٣٣) وَبَدا لَهُمْ ** (The evil deeds they had done became manifest to them,) it became manifest to them سَيِّئاتُ ما عَمِلُوا ** (The evil deeds they had done became manifest to them,) by knowing its ugliness (قبحها) and witnessing the grievousness (وخامة) of its consequence, وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (and they were besieged by what they used to ridicule.) which is the recompense (الجزاء). (٣٤) وَقِيلَ الْيَوْمَ نَنْساكُمْ ** (And it will be said, 'Today We forget you,) We abandon you in the punishment, the abandonment of that which is forgotten كَما نَسِيتُمْ لِقاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هذا ** (just as you forgot the encounter of this day of yours.) just as you abandoned preparation for it (عدّته) and did not care about it. وَمَأْواكُمُ النَّارُ وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ ناصِرِينَ** (Your refuge is the Fire, and you will have no helpers.') who can save you from it.

(٣٥) ذلِكُمْ بِأَنَّكُمُ اتَّخَذْتُمْ آياتِ اللهِ هُزُواً ** (35) That is because you took the signs of Allah in derision, Al-Qummi (said): and they are the Imams (peace be upon them), meaning you denied them and mocked them. وَغَرَّتْكُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا ** and the life of the world deceived you. So you assumed that there is no life besides it. فَالْيَوْمَ لا يُخْرَجُونَ مِنْها So today they will not be brought out of it, (meaning) from the Fire. And it was recited with the opening (فتح) of the yā' (الياء) and the ḍammah (ضمّ) of the rā' (الرّاء). **وَلا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ ** nor will they be asked to appease [their Lord]. It is not requested of them that they should appease their Lord, meaning that they should satisfy Him, due to the passing of its time. And Al-Qummi (said): nor will they be answered, nor will Allah accept them.

(٣٦) فَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ رَبِّ السَّماواتِ وَرَبِّ الْأَرْضِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (36) All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the heavens and the Lord of the earth, the Lord of all the worlds. Since everything is a blessing from Him. (٣٧) وَلَهُ الْكِبْرِياءُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (37) To Him belongs the majesty in the heavens and the earth, since the traces of His power (آثار قدرته) have appeared therein. In the الحديث القدسي (Sacred Narration): "Majesty (الكبرياء) is My cloak (ردائي) and Greatness (العظمة) is My wrapper (ازاري). Whoever contends with Me concerning one of them, I shall cast him into the Fire of Hell (نار جهنّم)." وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** the One Who is not overcome الْحَكِيمُ ** the All-wise. in what He decreed (قدّر) and ordained (قضى). So praise Him, magnify Him, and obey Him.

In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Surah al-Jathiyah, its reward will be that he shall never see the Fire (النار) and shall never hear the roaring (زفير) or the gasping (شهيقها) of Hell (جهنّم), and he shall be with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.
